project_name: WebSocket Origin Validation for Audit Event Stream
summary: Fix security vulnerability in audit service WebSocket streamer by implementing proper Origin header validation. The current implementation allows any origin to connect, exposing the platform to cross-site WebSocket hijacking (CSWSH) attacks where malicious sites can establish connections to the audit stream and potentially intercept sensitive audit events.

user_stories.i_want: to configure a whitelist of allowed origins for WebSocket connections to the audit stream
user_stories.so_that: only authorized domains can establish WebSocket connections and receive audit event data
acceptance_criteria: Configuration AUDIT_ALLOWED_ORIGINS accepts comma-separated list of allowed origins
Origin header from WebSocket handshake is validated against the whitelist
Connections from unauthorized origins are rejected with HTTP 403 Forbidden
Default allows localhost for development, requires explicit config for production
Origins support schemes (http/https), hostnames, and optional ports

user_stories.i_want: the audit service to enforce origin validation in production by default
user_stories.so_that: production deployments are protected against cross-site WebSocket hijacking
acceptance_criteria: Production validation fails if AUDIT_ALLOWED_ORIGINS is not explicitly configured
Warning logged at startup if using permissive default in development
Configuration is loaded via OPENIDX_AUDIT_ALLOWED_ORIGINS or AUDIT_ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var

user_stories.i_want: origin validation failures to be logged with relevant details
user_stories.so_that: potential attacks can be detected and investigated
acceptance_criteria: Rejected connections log the origin, remote address, and timestamp
Log level is warning for repeated rejections from same source
Successful connections log the approved origin

functional_requirements.title: Origin Header Validation
functional_requirements.description: Parse the Origin header from incoming WebSocket handshake requests and validate against a configurable whitelist. The validation must check for exact match including scheme, hostname, and optional port. Subdomain wildcard support (e.g., *.example.com) should be considered for future enhancement.

functional_requirements.title: Configuration Management
functional_requirements.description: Add AUDIT_ALLOWED_ORIGINS configuration option to the Config struct. Support comma-separated list of origin URLs. Parse the list into a slice for efficient validation during WebSocket upgrade. Empty list should reject all origins; '*' (if explicitly set) allows all origins with warning.

functional_requirements.title: Environment Variable Binding
functional_requirements.description: Bind AUDIT_ALLOWED_ORIGINS to environment variables OPENIDX_AUDIT_ALLOWED_ORIGINS and AUDIT_ALLOWED_ORIGINS for flexible deployment configuration. Follow the existing Viper configuration pattern used throughout the codebase.

functional_requirements.title: Production Safety Validation
functional_requirements.description: Add production validation for audit WebSocket origins. In production mode, AUDIT_ALLOWED_ORIGINS must be explicitly set and cannot be empty or use wildcard '*'. This validation should integrate with existing Config.ValidateProduction() method.

functional_requirements.title: WebSocket Upgrader Configuration
functional_requirements.description: Replace the current CheckOrigin function that returns true with a proper validation function that closes over the allowed origins list from configuration. The upgrader must be initialized with the configured origins.

functional_requirements.title: Origin Parsing and Normalization
functional_requirements.description: Implement robust origin URL parsing that handles various formats including with/without ports, different schemes (ws://, wss://, http://, https://). Normalize origins before comparison to avoid bypass via slight variations.

functional_requirements.title: FIDO2/WebAuthn Passkey Support
functional_requirements.description: Enable passwordless authentication using platform and roaming passkeys. Support FaceID, Windows Hello, YubiKey.
functional_requirements.competitive_advantage: Meet modern security expectations, eliminate password-related vulnerabilities

functional_requirements.title: Conditional Access Engine
functional_requirements.description: Real-time policy evaluation based on user risk, location, device, and context. Integration with threat intelligence.
functional_requirements.competitive_advantage: Core Zero Trust capability, matches Entra ID/Okta differentiator

functional_requirements.title: Identity Protection Service
functional_requirements.description: ML-based anomaly detection for login patterns, impossible travel, credential stuffing. Automated remediation.
functional_requirements.competitive_advantage: Proactive security vs reactive, high-value enterprise feature

functional_requirements.title: Device Trust Integration
functional_requirements.description: Assess device health, OS version, patch level, and security posture before granting access. Integration with MDM/EDR.
functional_requirements.competitive_advantage: Complete Zero Trust coverage, addresses growing BYOD security needs

functional_requirements.title: Privileged Identity Management (PIM)
functional_requirements.description: Just-in-time privilege elevation, approval workflows, access reviews for privileged roles. Audit trail.
functional_requirements.competitive_advantage: Enterprise must-have, high margin feature, addresses least privilege

functional_requirements.title: Advanced MFA Methods
functional_requirements.description: Add TOTP, hardware keys (YubiKey), biometric auth, push notifications with number matching.
functional_requirements.competitive_advantage: Flexibility for different security requirements and user preferences

functional_requirements.title: Access Request Catalog
functional_requirements.description: Self-service access request portal with approval workflows, RBAC, request justification, and SLA tracking.
functional_requirements.competitive_advantage: Reduces IT overhead, improves user experience, governance essential

functional_requirements.title: Automated Access Certification
functional_requirements.description: Periodic access review campaigns, manager attestation, orphan account detection, compliance evidence generation.
functional_requirements.competitive_advantage: Compliance requirement for regulated industries, audit readiness

functional_requirements.title: Self-Service User Portal
functional_requirements.description: User profile management, security settings, MFA enrollment, activity review, app access.
functional_requirements.competitive_advantage: Reduces helpdesk calls, modern user experience

non_functional_requirements.metric: All WebSocket connections from unauthorized origins must be rejected before upgrade

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Origin validation must not introduce significant latency
Configuration validation prevents insecure defaults in production
non_functional_requirements.metric: Service startup fails with clear error if production configuration is insecure
Browser-enforced Origin header is used; user-controlled headers are ignored
All rejection events logged with origin and source IP for SOC monitoring

api_endpoints.description: WebSocket endpoint for real-time audit event streaming. Origin header validation applied during WebSocket upgrade handshake.

risks.risk: Breaking existing WebSocket clients after deployment
risks.mitigation: Communicate configuration requirements to operators; include current clients in default whitelist during transition period

risks.risk: Origin header may be absent in some WebSocket clients
risks.mitigation: Reject connections without Origin header as unsafe; require explicit client configuration

risks.risk: Configuration error causing service startup failure in production
risks.mitigation: Provide clear error messages indicating required configuration; include migration guide

risks.risk: Subdomain bypass if exact matching is implemented without normalization
risks.mitigation: Implement proper URL parsing and normalization; consider future wildcard support

implementation_phases.name: Configuration Infrastructure
tasks: Add AuditAllowedOrigins field to Config struct with mapstructure tag
Add default value for development (localhost origins)
Add environment variable binding for OPENIDX_AUDIT_ALLOWED_ORIGINS
Add production validation in ValidateProduction() method

implementation_phases.name: Origin Validation Logic
tasks: Implement URL parsing and normalization for origins
Add exact match comparison with allowed origins list
Add logging for validation failures (origin, IP, timestamp)
Add logging for successful connections with approved origin

implementation_phases.name: WebSocket Upgrader Integration
tasks: Create dynamic upgrader with configured origins in NewEventStreamer
Replace static upgrader variable with instance-specific upgrader
Pass allowed origins to CheckOrigin closure

implementation_phases.name: Testing and Documentation
tasks: Add unit tests for origin validation with various formats
Add unit tests for production validation
Add integration test for WebSocket connection rejection
Add security advisory note about required configuration

key_gaps: AI-powered identity threat detection and automated response
Device trust assessment and posture checking
Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA) for application access
unique_selling_points: Open-source with full transparency and no vendor lock-in
Built-in OPA policy engine for flexible authorization
API Gateway (APISIX) integration for edge security
Modern tech stack (Go, React, PostgreSQL) with active development
Self-hosted option for data sovereignty and compliance requirements
No per-user pricing - predictable infrastructure costs
